summary: Large tournament-mode decompilation pass for src/melee/gm/gmtou.c plus a corrected TmAnimTimers layout in gmtou.static.h. The PR replaced multiple placeholder markers with C for tournament character/slot UI setup, per-frame input handling, scene entry, result/bracket processing, fog/text/model setup, and several JObj animation callbacks. decomp.dev reported 4 new full matches, including fn_8019BA08, fn_8019C744, fn_8019DD60, and gmtou .bss, plus major partial progress on fn_8019D1BC, gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame, gm_8019E634, fn_8019C048, and gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter.

key_files.role: Main tournament-mode decompilation pass; added includes, externs, helper struct declarations, full/partial implementations, and archive/global fixes.
Adjusted TmAnimTimers field layout to support per-player animation/job state fields used by new gmtou.c code and complete .bss matching.

what_changed: Changed extern TmBoxArrays* lbl_804771B8 to extern TmBoxArrays lbl_804771B8 and updated TmBox.dat archive symbol arguments from lbl_804771B8->boxN to &lbl_804771B8.boxN.
Added required includes for fog, gobj gx link/object setup, OS, dobj, mobj, gm_1601, gm_1A3F, gm_1A45, and gmmain_lib dependencies.
Added extern declarations for lbl_804D663C, lbl_803DA2E0, gm_80477738, lbl_803B7D3C, lbl_804DA948, fn_80166CBC, and lbl_803B7D18.
Introduced struct lbl_803DA2E0_t with u8 x0[0x20] and f32 x20[0x29], used for tournament icon/state lookup and cached translation-Y animation samples.
Implemented fn_8019BA08, which walks child/next JObj siblings and toggles visibility by pushing unused entries to translate Z 10000.0f, using lbl_803DA2E0.x0 and tmd->x4B8 player state.

Implemented fn_8019C048 as a per-player UI animation callback handling controller-disconnect hiding, SIS text scale, X position by player count, frame ranges from lbl_803B7D18, and state transitions in lbl_80479A58.x1D.
Implemented fn_8019C744 as another per-player JObj callback, including position setup, falling/bounce Y translation from lbl_803DA2E0.x20 while state is 4, and disconnect visibility handling.

Implemented fn_8019D1BC to create many tournament UI GObjs from lbl_804D6694 models, bind per-player indices with fn_8018FBD8, remove selected material animations, position icons/names, cache animation Y samples, and create SIS text for bracket names.
Implemented fn_8019DD60 to initialize the tournament scene camera, lights, model callbacks, SIS text object, fog GObj, audio cue, and TmAnimTimers fields.

Implemented large portions of gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame for tournament selection input: menu cancel path, all-player confirmation timer, abort-combo handling, character cycling, random/CPU state toggling, color changes, VS data creation, and fn_8019EE80 updates.

Implemented large portions of gm_8019E634 for tournament result sorting, handicap adjustment, bracket slot reassignment, copying x37 data into x4B8 active-player entries, OSReport debug output, and audio preload masking.
Implemented large portions of gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter to load GmTou1p/GmTou4p archives, initialize SIS_TournamentData, set up scene/audio, compute stage/character audio masks, and preload resources.
Updated existing references from lbl_80479A58.x20[idx].x0 to lbl_80479A58.x1D[idx].x0 after the TmAnimTimers layout correction.

Reworked TmAnimTimers in gmtou.static.h: x0 became u32, x6 became u16[4], x10[4] and x18[4] were exposed, per-player x1D[4] entries gained a/b/c frame fields plus x0/x1/x2 state fields, and x38[4] was added.
smart_moves: The TmAnimTimers relayout was not just cosmetic: the bot report shows gmtou .bss improved from 50.00% to 100.00%, indicating the corrected struct field sizes and offsets unlocked data matching.

Correcting lbl_804771B8 from pointer-to-struct to struct object fixed archive symbol argument generation by taking addresses of box2/box3/box4 fields, a common decomp pattern when a data symbol is an aggregate rather than a runtime pointer.

The implementation preserved compiler-sensitive structure with explicit local table copies from lbl_803B7D18, u16 casts over a local s32[9] table, narrow u8 temporaries, and signed pad-error casts, all of which are likely matching-driven choices.
Using PAD_STACK in several large functions documents stack-padding requirements without obscuring the reconstructed control flow.

The code models asset/UI setup through the existing engine APIs instead of handwaving placeholders: fn_8019035C for model GObj creation, GET_JOBJ/JObj traversal, HSD_MObjRemoveAnimByFlags, SIS text initialization, and fog GObj setup are all wired through actual baselib calls.

Caching Y translations from an animation JObj into lbl_803DA2E0.x20 in fn_8019D1BC gives fn_8019C744 a data-driven lookup table for later per-frame movement, matching the observed global data use rather than recomputing or inventing a formula.
Repeated player-count position formulas were kept in the same float shape and constants seen throughout the file, which helps preserve codegen for 2-, 3-, and 4-player tournament layouts.
decomp_lessons: For static data referenced through archive loading, verify whether the symbol is the aggregate itself or a pointer before encoding extern types; the difference changes callsite address-taking and can affect matching.

When a BSS struct has per-player state, reconstruct field offsets from all consumers before naming fields. Here, x1D[4], x10[4], x18[4], and x38[4] were exposed only after new callbacks showed how the data was indexed.
Tournament UI code heavily reuses anonymous/unknown TmData fields; stable offset names like x4B8, x37, x524, and x534 can still support matching when semantic names are not yet evidence-backed.
Controller error checks may require exact signed/narrow casts such as (s8)(u8) HSD_PadMasterStatus[idx].err to match the original codegen.
Local copies of global data tables can be necessary even when they look redundant; fn_8019C048 copies lbl_803B7D18 into a stack table and then treats it as u16 frame triples.

Large UI initialization functions can be decomposed by preserving creation order, child traversal order, model indices, callback assignments, and literal float constants before trying to improve names.
Use placeholder comments only as boundaries; this PR shows a productive pattern of replacing /// #function markers with partially matching C while keeping remaining unknown field names conservative.
Bot match reports are useful evidence for prioritizing follow-up work: functions at 90-99% likely need small stack/control-flow/type fixes rather than wholesale rewrites.
naming_conventions: Newly implemented unmatched functions retained address-based names such as fn_8019BA08, fn_8019C048, fn_8019C744, fn_8019D1BC, fn_8019DD60, gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame, gm_8019E634, and gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter.
Scene lifecycle functions use the existing gm address plus suffix convention: OnEnter, OnFrame, and OnLeave.
Unidentified globals remain lbl_-address names, including lbl_80479A58, lbl_803DA2E0, lbl_803B7D18, lbl_803B7D3C, lbl_804D663C, and lbl_804DA948.
Struct fields without confirmed semantics keep offset-style names such as TmData x4B8, x37, x524, x534 and TmAnimTimers x1D, x10, x18, x38.

Where minimal semantics were evident, local names were introduced conservatively: tmd, gobj, jobj, c, idx, pos, anim, confirmed, buttons, pressed, start_frame, cur_frame, end_frame, loop_flag, name_buf, audio_mask.
The TmAnimTimers nested per-player entry uses a/b/c for frame range fields and x0/x1/x2 for state/flag fields, with the comment updated to 'per-player jobj/anim states'.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0xA0), PAD_STACK(16), and PAD_STACK(4) in functions with stack layout sensitivity.
Preserved goto-based search blocks such as left_found, right_found, down_found, up_found, and found1-found13 to match original control flow for loops that search TmData x37 entries.
Used explicit casts to force codegen, including (s32) tmd->x30, (u8) idx, (s8)(u8) pad err, (f32) indices, and casting local s32 table storage to u16* for frame triples.
Kept exact float literals and arithmetic forms such as 13.0f, -19.5f, 6.5f, 73.0f, 240.0f, 480.0f, 0.99f * (pos - 320.0f) - 261.0f, 5.999997f, 2.799999f, 666.0f, and 0.001f.
Copied global arrays into locals before use: lbl_803B7D18 into table[9] and lbl_803B7D3C into indices[4].
Used object traversal calls in repeated explicit steps rather than compact loops where that likely matched original unrolled child/next access patterns.
Retained repeated gm_8018F634 calls inside lookup blocks, suggesting the extra calls were needed for match rather than hoisted for readability.
Used exact model indices and callback wiring in fn_8019D1BC/fn_8019DD60, preserving scene construction order.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code increased to 64.43% (+0.07%, +2608 bytes) and matched data increased to 38.39% (+0.01%, +64 bytes).
New 100% matches reported for main/melee/gm/gmtou fn_8019BA08 (+1296 bytes), fn_8019C744 (+756 bytes), fn_8019DD60 (+556 bytes), and .bss (+32 bytes).

Partial improvements reported for fn_8019D1BC to 98.71%, gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame to 97.14%, gm_8019E634 to 90.94%, fn_8019C048 to 84.12%, gm_8019ECAC_OnEnter to 98.98%, plus .sdata2, gm_8019B8C4_OnEnter, .data, and .sdata.
No human review comments or PR body rationale were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Investigate the remaining mismatch in fn_8019D1BC, which decomp.dev reported at 98.71% after this PR.
Investigate the remaining mismatch in gm_8019DF8C_OnFrame, especially goto/search-block and input-handling codegen, reported at 97.14%.
Investigate gm_8019E634 result/bracket processing, reported at 90.94%, for possible struct field typing and loop-shape improvements.
Refine semantic names for TmAnimTimers.x1D fields a/b/c/x0/x1/x2 once more tournament animation uses are understood.
Refine semantic names for TmData x4B8, x37, x524, and x534 fields used by tournament character slots, bracket entries, and SIS text objects.
Verify whether lbl_803DA2E0_t has a known data-table name or can be moved to a shared/static header once more references are identified.
